revision.text: #redirect[[en:壬]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{十干}} '''壬'''（じん、みずのえ）は、[[十干]]の9番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[水]]性の陽に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「みずのえ」（水の兄）ともいう。
壬の字は「妊」につながり、植物の内部に種子が生まれた状態として、9番目に宛てられた。
==壬を含む干支== *[[壬申]] *[[壬午]] *[[壬辰]] *[[壬寅]] *[[壬子]] *[[壬戌]]
{{stub}} [[Category:干支|みすのえ]]

revision.text:
{{十干十二支}} '''Ren''' (壬) is the 9th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=4d707a1e4dd930dc3b4490eea48871fc708a7245ce283f1e60d71f2dc1d03e10JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Mizunoe+(%e5%a3%ac)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NjAxNyZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxOTU5&ntb=1</ref>
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}} correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as {{ill|nonane|ja|ノナン}} and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mizunoe (壬)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:壬]]
==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mizunoe (壬)]][[Category:11]] {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Water Monkey|ja|壬申|zh|壬申|ko|임신|fr|Singe d'eau|de|Wasser-Affe|en|Water Monkey|lt=壬申 (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|壬午|zh|壬午|ko|임오|fr|Cheval d'eau|de|Wasser-Pferd|en|Water Horse|lt=壬午 (Yang Water Horse)|lt_ko=임오}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dragon|ja|壬辰|zh|壬辰|ko|임진|fr|Dragon d'eau|de|Wasser-Drache|en|Water Dragon|lt=壬辰 (Yang Water Dragon)|lt_ko=임진}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Tiger|ja|壬寅|zh|壬寅|ko|임인|fr|Tigre d'eau|de|Wasser-Tiger|en|Water Tiger|lt=壬寅 (Yang Water Tiger)|lt_ko=임인}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Rat|ja|壬子|zh|壬子|ko|임자|fr|Rat d'eau|de|Wasser-Ratte|en|Water Rat|lt=壬子 (Yang Water Rat)|lt_ko=임자}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dog|ja|壬戌|zh|壬戌|ko|임술|fr|Chien d'eau|de|Wasser-Hund|en|Water Dog|lt=壬戌 (Yang Water Dog)|lt_ko=임술}} |} == See Also == * [[Jinshin War]] * {{prefix}} * {{intitle}}

{{十干十二支}} '''Ren''' (壬) is the 9th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=4d707a1e4dd930dc3b4490eea48871fc708a7245ce283f1e60d71f2dc1d03e10JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Mizunoe+(%e5%a3%ac)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NjAxNyZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxOTU5&ntb=1</ref>
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}} correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as {{ill|nonane|ja|ノナン}} and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees).

{{十干十二支}} '''Ren''' (壬) is the 9th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=4d707a1e4dd930dc3b4490eea48871fc708a7245ce283f1e60d71f2dc1d03e10JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Mizunoe+(%e5%a3%ac)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NjAxNyZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxOTU5&ntb=1</ref>
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}} correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as {{ill|nonane|ja|ノナン}} and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Ìng (tiĕng-găng)]][[commons:Category:壬 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Ren (tige céleste)]][[gan:壬]][[hak:Ngìm (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:壬]][[ko:임 (천간)]][[vi:Nhâm (Thiên can)]][[za:Nyaemz (diengan)]][[zh_min_nan:Jîm (Thian-kan)]][[zh_yue:壬 (天干)]][[zh:壬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1078386]] Wikidata name: rén [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoe''' (壬) is the 9th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=4d707a1e4dd930dc3b4490eea48871fc708a7245ce283f1e60d71f2dc1d03e10JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Mizunoe+(%e5%a3%ac)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NjAxNyZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxOTU5&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== {{wiktionary|壬}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Water Monkey|ja|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|zh|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|ko|임신|fr|Singe d'eau|de|Wasser-Affe|en|Water Monkey|lt=壬申 (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Monkey|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|zh|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|ko|임오|fr|Cheval d'eau|de|Wasser-Pferd|en|Water Horse|lt=壬午 (Yang Water Horse)|lt_ko=임오|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Horse|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dragon|ja|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|zh|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|ko|임진|fr|Dragon d'eau|de|Wasser-Drache|en|Water Dragon|lt=壬辰 (Yang Water Dragon)|lt_ko=임진|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dragon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Tiger|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|zh|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|ko|임인|fr|Tigre d'eau|de|Wasser-Tiger|en|Water Tiger|lt=壬寅 (Yang Water Tiger)|lt_ko=임인|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Tiger|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Rat|ja|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|zh|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|ko|임자|fr|Rat d'eau|de|Wasser-Ratte|en|Water Rat|lt=壬子 (Yang Water Rat)|lt_ko=임자|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dog|ja|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|zh|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|ko|임술|fr|Chien d'eau|de|Wasser-Hund|en|Water Dog|lt=壬戌 (Yang Water Dog)|lt_ko=임술|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |}

== Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== {{wiktionary|壬}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Water Monkey|ja|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|zh|1=Yang Water Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== {{wiktionary|壬}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Water Monkey|ja|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|zh|1=Yang Water == Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|1=Yang Water == Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== {{wiktionary|壬}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Water Monkey|ja|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|zh|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|ko|임신|fr|Singe d'eau|de|Wasser-Affe|en|Water Monkey|lt=壬申 (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|zh|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|ko|임오|fr|Cheval d'eau|de|Wasser-Pferd|en|Water Horse|lt=壬午 (Yang Water Horse)|lt_ko=임오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Horse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dragon|ja|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|zh|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|ko|임진|fr|Dragon d'eau|de|Wasser-Drache|en|Water Dragon|lt=壬辰 (Yang Water Dragon)|lt_ko=임진|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Tiger|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|zh|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|ko|임인|fr|Tigre d'eau|de|Wasser-Tiger|en|Water Tiger|lt=壬寅 (Yang Water Tiger)|lt_ko=임인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Tiger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Rat|ja|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|zh|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de == Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== == Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Ìng (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:壬 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Ren (tige céleste)]][[gan:壬]][[hak:Ngìm (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:壬]][[ko:임 (천간)]][[vi:Nhâm (Thiên can)]][[za:Nyaemz (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Jîm (Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:壬 (天干)]][[zh:壬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078386]] Wikidata name: rén [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoe''' (壬) is the 9th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=4d707a1e4dd930dc3b4490eea48871fc708a7245ce283f1e60d71f2dc1d03e10JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Mizunoe+(%e5%a3%ac)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NjAxNyZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxOTU5&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== {{wiktionary|壬}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Water Monkey|ja|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|zh|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|ko|임신|fr|Singe d'eau|de|Wasser-Affe|en|Water Monkey|lt=壬申 (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|zh|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|ko|임오|fr|Cheval d'eau|de|Wasser-Pferd|en|Water Horse|lt=壬午 (Yang Water Horse)|lt_ko=임오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Horse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dragon|ja|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|zh|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|ko|임진|fr|Dragon d'eau|de|Wasser-Drache|en|Water Dragon|lt=壬辰 (Yang Water Dragon)|lt_ko=임진|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Tiger|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|zh|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|ko|임인|fr|Tigre d'eau|de|Wasser-Tiger|en|Water Tiger|lt=壬寅 (Yang Water Tiger)|lt_ko=임인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Tiger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Rat|ja|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|zh|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|ko|임자|fr|Rat d'eau|de|Wasser-Ratte|en|Water Rat|lt=壬子 (Yang Water Rat)|lt_ko=임자|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dog|ja|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|zh|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|ko|임술|fr|Chien d'eau|de|Wasser-Hund|en|Water Dog|lt=壬戌 (Yang Water Dog)|lt_ko=임술|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Ìng (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:壬 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Ren (tige céleste)]][[gan:壬]][[hak:Ngìm (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:壬]][[ko:임 (천간)]][[vi:Nhâm (Thiên can)]][[za:Nyaemz (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Jîm (Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:壬 (天干)]][[zh:壬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078386]] Wikidata name: rén [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoe''' (壬) is the 9th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=4d707a1e4dd930dc3b4490eea48871fc708a7245ce283f1e60d71f2dc1d03e10JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Mizunoe+(%e5%a3%ac)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NjAxNyZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxOTU5&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== {{wiktionary|壬}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Water Monkey|ja|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|zh|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|ko|임신|fr|Singe d'eau|de|Wasser-Affe|en|Water Monkey|lt=壬申 (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|zh|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|ko|임오|fr|Cheval d'eau|de|Wasser-Pferd|en|Water Horse|lt=壬午 (Yang Water Horse)|lt_ko=임오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Horse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dragon|ja|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|zh|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|ko|임진|fr|Dragon d'eau|de|Wasser-Drache|en|Water Dragon|lt=壬辰 (Yang Water Dragon)|lt_ko=임진|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Tiger|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|zh|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|ko|임인|fr|Tigre d'eau|de|Wasser-Tiger|en|Water Tiger|lt=壬寅 (Yang Water Tiger)|lt_ko=임인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Tiger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Rat|ja|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|zh|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de Rat|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dog|ja|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|zh|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|ko|임술|fr|Chien d'eau|de|Wasser-Hund|en|Water Dog|lt=壬戌 (Yang Water == Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== == Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== {{wiktionary|壬}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Water Monkey|ja|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|zh|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|ko|임신|fr|Singe d'eau|de|Wasser-Affe|en|Water Monkey|lt=壬申 (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|zh|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|ko|임오|fr|Cheval d'eau|de|Wasser-Pferd|en|Water Horse|lt=壬午 (Yang Water Horse)|lt_ko=임오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Horse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dragon|ja|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|zh|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|ko|임진|fr|Dragon d'eau|de|Wasser-Drache|en|Water Dragon|lt=壬辰 (Yang Water Dragon)|lt_ko=임진|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Tiger|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|zh|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|ko|임인|fr|Tigre d'eau|de|Wasser-Tiger|en|Water Tiger|lt=壬寅 (Yang Water Tiger)|lt_ko=임인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Tiger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Rat|ja|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|zh|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de == Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== {{wiktionary|壬}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Water Monkey|ja|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|zh|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|ko|임신|fr|Singe d'eau|de|Wasser-Affe|en|Water Monkey|lt=壬申 (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|zh|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|ko|임오|fr|Cheval d'eau|de|Wasser-Pferd|en|Water Horse|lt=壬午 (Yang Water Horse)|lt_ko=임오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Horse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dragon|ja|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|zh|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|ko|임진|fr|Dragon d'eau|de|Wasser-Drache|en|Water Dragon|lt=壬辰 (Yang Water Dragon)|lt_ko=임진|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Tiger|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|zh|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|ko|임인|fr|Tigre d'eau|de|Wasser-Tiger|en|Water Tiger|lt=壬寅 (Yang Water Tiger)|lt_ko=임인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Tiger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Rat|ja|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|zh|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|ko|임자|fr|Rat d'eau|de|Wasser-Ratte|en|Water Rat|lt=壬子 (Yang Water Rat)|lt_ko=임자|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dog|ja|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|zh|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|ko|임술|fr|Chien d'eau|de|Wasser-Hund|en|Water Dog|lt=壬戌 (Yang Water Dog)|lt_ko=임술|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |}

|url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== == Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|zh|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|ko|임오|fr|Cheval d'eau|de|Wasser-Pferd|en|Water Horse|lt=壬午 (Yang Water Horse)|lt_ko=임오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Horse|ja_comment=jawiki link == Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Nonane|nonane]] and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|zh|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|ko|임오|fr|Cheval d'eau|de|Wasser-Pferd|en|Water Horse|lt=壬午 (Yang Water Horse)|lt_ko=임오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Horse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dragon|ja|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|zh|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|ko|임진|fr|Dragon d'eau|de|Wasser-Drache|en|Water Dragon|lt=壬辰 (Yang Water Dragon)|lt_ko=임진|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Tiger|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|zh|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|ko|임인|fr|Tigre d'eau|de|Wasser-Tiger|en|Water Tiger|lt=壬寅 (Yang Water Tiger)|lt_ko=임인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Tiger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Rat|ja|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|zh|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de == Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era}} correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as {{ill|Nonane|en|Nonane|WD=Q150694|lt=nonane}} and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== {{wiktionary|壬}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Water Monkey|ja|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|zh|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|ko|임신|fr|Singe d'eau|de|Wasser-Affe|en|Water Monkey|lt=壬申 (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|zh|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|ko|임오|fr|Cheval d'eau|de|Wasser-Pferd|en|Water Horse|lt=壬午 (Yang Water Horse)|lt_ko=임오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Horse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dragon|ja|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|zh|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|ko|임진|fr|Dragon d'eau|de|Wasser-Drache|en|Water Dragon|lt=壬辰 (Yang Water Dragon)|lt_ko=임진|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Tiger|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|zh|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|ko|임인|fr|Tigre d'eau|de|Wasser-Tiger|en|Water Tiger|lt=壬寅 (Yang Water Tiger)|lt_ko=임인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Tiger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Rat|ja|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoe''' (壬) is the 9th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=4d707a1e4dd930dc3b4490eea48871fc708a7245ce283f1e60d71f2dc1d03e10JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Mizunoe+(%e5%a3%ac)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NjAxNyZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxOTU5&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era}} correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as {{ill|Nonane|en|Nonane|WD=Q150694|lt=nonane}} and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== {{wiktionary|壬}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Water Monkey|ja|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|zh|1=Yang Water Monkey|2=ja|壬申|ko|임신|fr|Singe d'eau|de|Wasser-Affe|en|Water Monkey|lt=壬申 (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|zh|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|ko|임오|fr|Cheval d'eau|de|Wasser-Pferd|en|Water Horse|lt=壬午 (Yang Water Horse)|lt_ko=임오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Horse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dragon|ja|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|zh|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|ko|임진|fr|Dragon d'eau|de|Wasser-Drache|en|Water Dragon|lt=壬辰 (Yang Water Dragon)|lt_ko=임진|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Tiger|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|zh|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|ko|임인|fr|Tigre d'eau|de|Wasser-Tiger|en|Water Tiger|lt=壬寅 (Yang Water Tiger)|lt_ko=임인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Tiger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Rat|ja|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|zh|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|ko|임자|fr|Rat d'eau|de|Wasser-Ratte|en|Water Rat|lt=壬子 (Yang Water Rat)|lt_ko=임자|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dog|ja|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|zh|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|ko|임술|fr|Chien d'eau|de|Wasser-Hund|en|Water Dog|lt=壬戌 (Yang Water Dog)|lt_ko=임술|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |}

|url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as {{ill|Nonane|en|Nonane|WD=Q150694|lt=nonane}} and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoe''' (壬) is the 9th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|1=Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=4d707a1e4dd930dc3b4490eea48871fc708a7245ce283f1e60d71f2dc1d03e10JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Mizunoe+(%e5%a3%ac)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NjAxNyZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxOTU5&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era|1=Common Era}} correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=Functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as {{ill|Nonane|en|Nonane|WD=Q150694|lt=nonane|1=Nonane}} and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|zh|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|ko|임오|fr|Cheval d'eau|de|Wasser-Pferd|en|Water Horse|lt=壬午 (Yang Water Horse)|lt_ko=임오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Horse|ja_comment=jawiki link == Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era|1=Common Era}} correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=Functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as {{ill|Nonane|en|Nonane|WD=Q150694|lt=nonane|1=Nonane}} and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)== (Yang Water Monkey)|lt_ko=임신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Horse|ja|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|zh|1=Yang Water Horse|2=ja|壬午|ko|임오|fr|Cheval d'eau|de|Wasser-Pferd|en|Water Horse|lt=壬午 (Yang Water Horse)|lt_ko=임오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Horse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Dragon|ja|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|zh|1=Yang Water Dragon|2=ja|壬辰|ko|임진|fr|Dragon d'eau|de|Wasser-Drache|en|Water Dragon|lt=壬辰 (Yang Water Dragon)|lt_ko=임진|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Tiger|ja|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|zh|1=Yang Water Tiger|2=ja|壬寅|ko|임인|fr|Tigre d'eau|de|Wasser-Tiger|en|Water Tiger|lt=壬寅 (Yang Water Tiger)|lt_ko=임인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Tiger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Water Rat|ja|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|zh|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoe''' (壬) is the 9th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|qid=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|1=Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=4d707a1e4dd930dc3b4490eea48871fc708a7245ce283f1e60d71f2dc1d03e10JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Mizunoe+(%e5%a3%ac)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NjAxNyZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxOTU5&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 壬 (Stem 9) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Rén * Korean: Im (임) * Vietnamese: Nhâm * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Jin (じん) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoe (みずのえ, 水の兄 – “Elder Water”)
== Overview == * The origin of the Chinese character "壬" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reappearance of Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=110–11|qq=}}</ref> It is used as a borrowed character to denote the ninth of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * Years ending with the digit 2 in the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|qid=Q208141|lt=Common Era|1=Common Era}} correspond to the {{ill|Ren|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ren|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoe (壬)|qq=}} year. Additionally, when representing the year, it is also known by the name '''Xuanyi''' (玄黓).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternate Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20826683}}, "Ren" is used in the names of compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=Functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q170409}} containing nine carbon atoms, such as {{ill|Nonane|en|Nonane|qid=Q150694|lt=nonane|1=Nonane}} and {{ill|nonyl group|ja|ノニル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nonyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The counterpart of Ren is {{ill|Ding|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoto (丁)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Ren is north-northwest, slightly north (345 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoe (壬)==
